message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: Concurrent setup() once-per-conn guard is asserted only sequentially, not under real threads
DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior
SEVERITY: LOW
FILE: tests/test_persistence.py 429-447
DESCRIPTION: checkpoint.py documents that the in-process _init_lock makes the bootstrap check-and-set atomic 'so concurrent threads don't both run setup' (checkpoint.py:22-23, 195). test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection calls get_checkpointer() twice SEQUENTIALLY, which exercises only the once-per-conn dedup, not the concurrency claim — two threads racing into the `if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS` window would still be serialized by GIL+narrow-window even without the lock, but the test cannot demonstrate the lock matters. Unlike the provenance concurrency test (test_provenance.py:170, which injects a sleeping clock to widen the race and was verified discriminating by removing the lock), there is no thread-level test that the checkpoint _init_lock is load-bearing.
SUGGESTED FIX: Add a threaded test that launches several get_checkpointer() calls concurrently (with the fake_pg backend, optionally a small sleep inside FakeSaver.setup to widen the window) and asserts setup() still ran exactly once. Verify it is discriminating by confirming it fails if the `with _init_lock:` around the bootstrap block is removed.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

401	        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, name, m)
402	        return m
403	
404	    _mod("langgraph")
405	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint")
406	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.postgres", PostgresSaver=FakeSaver)
407	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.memory", MemorySaver=type("MemorySaver", (), {}))
408	    _mod("psycopg")
409	    _mod("psycopg.rows", dict_row=object())
410	    _mod("psycopg_pool", ConnectionPool=FakePool)
411	
412	    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
413	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS", set())
414	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])
415	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_atexit_registered", False)
416	    monkeypatch.setattr(
417	        cp,
418	        "atexit",
419	        types.SimpleNamespace(
420	            register=lambda fn: state.__setitem__(
421	                "atexit_registered", state["atexit_registered"] + 1
422	            )
423	        ),
424	    )
425	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay")
426	    return cp, state
427	
428	
429	def test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection(fake_pg):
430	    cp, state = fake_pg
431	    cp.get_checkpointer()
432	    cp.get_checkpointer()  # second call, same conn_str
433	    # setup() ran exactly once despite two factory calls (once-per-conn guard)
434	    assert len(state["setup_on"]) == 1
435	    # the advisory lock and the DDL ran on the SAME connection object
436	    assert state["lock_on"] == state["setup_on"]
437	
438	
439	def test_atexit_pool_cleanup_registered_once(fake_pg):
440	    cp, state = fake_pg
441	    cp.get_checkpointer()
442	    cp.get_checkpointer()
443	    # two pools opened and tracked, but only one shared atexit handler registered
444	    assert len(state["pools"]) == 2
445	    assert len(cp._OPEN_POOLS) == 2
446	    assert state["atexit_registered"] == 1
447	
448	
449	def test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails(fake_pg):
450	    # #105: if schema bootstrap fails AFTER the pool opened, the pool must be closed and dropped
451	    # from the cleanup registry — not leaked.
452	    import sys
453	
454	    cp, state = fake_pg
455	    closed: list = []
456	    Pool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
457	    orig_close = Pool.close
458	    Pool.close = lambda self: (closed.append(self), orig_close(self))[1]  # record close
459	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
460	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
461	
462	    def boom(self):
463	        raise RuntimeError("setup failed (schema DDL)")
464	
465	    Saver.setup = boom
466	    try:
467	        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="connection failed"):
468	            cp.get_checkpointer()
469	        assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # a pool was opened
470	        assert closed and closed[0] is state["pools"][0]  # and it was closed
471	        assert cp._OPEN_POOLS == []  # and removed from the cleanup registry (no leak)
472	    finally:
473	        Pool.close = orig_close
474	        Saver.setup = orig_setup
475	
476	
477	def test_connection_failure_redacts_and_leaks_no_context(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
478	    # issue #P2: on failure the credential-bearing original must be retained as neither
479	    # __cause__ nor __context__, and the message must be redacted.

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping. The PG advisory lock serializes DDL
56	# across PROCESSES; this lock makes the in-process check-and-set sequences (atexit
57	# registration, the initialized-conn set) atomic so two threads can't double-register or
58	# double-setup.
59	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
60	
61	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
62	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
63	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
64	_atexit_registered = False
65	
66	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
67	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
68	# first-time cross-process race.
69	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
70	
71	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
72	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
73	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
74	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
75	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
76	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
77	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
78	
79	
80	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
81	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
82	
83	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
84	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
85	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
86	    """
87	    try:
88	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
89	        host = u.hostname or ""
90	        port = u.port
91	        scheme = u.scheme
92	        path = u.path
93	    except ValueError:
94	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
95	    if not host:
96	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
97	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
98	        host = f"[{host}]"
99	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
100	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
101	
102	
103	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
104	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
105	
106	    Layers, in order of precision:
107	
108	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
109	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
110	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
111	       *other* URI in the text;
112	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
113	
114	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
115	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
116	    """
117	    if conn_str:
118	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
119	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
120	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
121	
122	
123	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
124	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
125	
126	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
127	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
128	    """
129	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
130	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
131	
132	
133	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
134	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
135	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
136	    that can raise)."""
137	    try:
138	        pool.close()
139	    except Exception:
140	        pass
141	
142	
143	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
144	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
145	        _safe_close(p)
146	
147	
148	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    global _atexit_registered
150	    with _init_lock:
151	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
152	        if not _atexit_registered:
153	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
154	            _atexit_registered = True
155	
156	
157	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
158	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
159	
160	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
161	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
162	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
163	    or the extra is not installed.
164	    """
165	    try:
166	        if use_memory:
167	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
168	
169	            return MemorySaver()
170	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
171	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
172	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
173	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
174	        raise RuntimeError(
175	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
176	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
177	        ) from exc
178	
179	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
180	    failure: str | None = None
181	    pool: Any = None
182	    try:
183	        pool = ConnectionPool(
184	            conn_str,
185	            min_size=1,
186	            max_size=8,
187	            kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
188	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
189	            open=True,
190	        )
191	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
192	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
193	        # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
194	        # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
195	        with _init_lock:
196	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
197	                with pool.connection() as conn:
198	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
199	                    try:
200	                        # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
201	                        # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
202	                        # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
203	                        PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
204	                    finally:
205	                        # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
206	                        # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
207	                        try:
208	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
209	                        except Exception:
210	                            pass
211	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
212	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
213	    except Exception as exc:
214	        # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
215	        # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
216	        # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
217	        failure = (
218	            f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
219	            f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
220	            "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
221	        )
222	        # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
223	        # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
224	        if pool is not None:
225	            _safe_close(pool)
226	            with _init_lock:
227	                if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
228	                    _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
229	    raise RuntimeError(failure)
230	
231	
232	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
233	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
234	
235	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
236	    otherwise Postgres.
237	    """
238	    if use_memory is None:
239	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
240	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
241

content.text: Now let me look at the test fixtures (FakeSaver, FakePool) and the provenance concurrency test mentioned as the gold standard.

content.content:
340	
341	    fc = FakeClient()
342	    store = QdrantVectorStore("c", 2, client=fc)
343	    store.upsert([f"u{i}" for i in range(6)], TrackingVectors([[0.0, 1.0]] * 6), batch_size=2)
344	    # per-batch construction → 2,4,6 consumed at each send; all-up-front would be 6,6,6
345	    assert fc.accessed_at_call == [2, 4, 6]
346	
347	
348	# ---- hardened checkpointer invariants (fake backends injected, no Postgres) ----
349	
350	
351	@pytest.fixture
352	def fake_pg(monkeypatch):
353	    """Inject fake langgraph/psycopg modules so get_checkpointer runs the full hardened path
354	    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""
355	    import sys
356	    import types
357	
358	    from assay_engine.persistence import checkpoint as cp
359	
360	    state = {"setup_on": [], "lock_on": [], "pools": [], "atexit_registered": 0}
361	
362	    class FakeConn:
363	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
364	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
365	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))
366	
367	    class FakePool:
368	        check_connection = staticmethod(lambda *a, **k: None)
369	
370	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
371	            self.conn_str = conn_str
372	            self._conn = FakeConn()
373	            state["pools"].append(self)
374	
375	        def connection(self):
376	            conn = self._conn
377	
378	            class _Ctx:
379	                def __enter__(self_):
380	                    return conn
381	
382	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
383	                    return False
384	
385	            return _Ctx()
386	
387	        def close(self):
388	            pass
389	
390	    class FakeSaver:
391	        def __init__(self, arg):
392	            self.arg = arg
393	
394	        def setup(self):
395	            state["setup_on"].append(id(self.arg))
396	
397	    def _mod(name, **attrs):
398	        m = types.ModuleType(name)
399	        for k, v in attrs.items():

150	        @property
151	        def approved(self):  # property that raises
152	            raise ValueError("evil")
153	
154	    t = ProvenanceTrail()
155	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
156	        t.as_recorder()(Evil())
157	
158	
159	# ---- determinism ----
160	
161	
162	def test_deterministic_hashes_under_fixed_clock():
163	    a = ProvenanceTrail(clock=_fixed_clock())
164	    a.record("k", "s", v={"b": 2, "a": 1})
165	    b = ProvenanceTrail(clock=_fixed_clock())
166	    b.record("k", "s", v={"a": 1, "b": 2})
167	    assert a.entries[0].entry_hash == b.entries[0].entry_hash
168	
169	
170	def test_record_is_thread_safe_under_concurrency():
171	    # #114: concurrent record() must not corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain.
172	    # A naive concurrency test is non-discriminating under the GIL — the read->append window is
173	    # too narrow to interleave. We inject a clock that sleeps INSIDE that window (record() reads
174	    # seq/prev_hash, then calls the clock, then appends), so an UNLOCKED record() would let
175	    # threads read the same seq and corrupt the chain. With the lock (held across the whole
176	    # _record_locked, clock included) threads serialize and the chain stays intact. Verified
177	    # discriminating: removing the lock makes this fail (duplicate seqs / broken chain).
178	    import threading
179	    import time
180	
181	    def widening_clock():
182	        time.sleep(0.0005)  # widen the read->append window to force interleaving if unlocked
183	        return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
184	
185	    t = ProvenanceTrail(clock=widening_clock)
186	    n_threads, per = 8, 15
187	    barrier = threading.Barrier(n_threads)
188	
189	    def worker(w):
190	        barrier.wait()  # release all threads at once → maximal contention in the window
191	        for i in range(per):
192	            t.record("k", f"w{w}-{i}", w=w, i=i)
193	
194	    threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker, args=(w,)) for w in range(n_threads)]
195	    for th in threads:
196	        th.start()
197	    for th in threads:
198	        th.join()
199	    assert len(t) == n_threads * per
200	    t.verify()  # intact hash chain despite concurrent appends through a widened window
201	    assert [e.seq for e in t.entries] == list(range(n_threads * per))  # contiguous, no dupes/gaps
202

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Let me verify the claim by writing a PoC: a threaded test against the fake backend, and check whether removing the lock makes it fail.

The assessor's core claim: there is no thread-level test demonstrating that the checkpoint `_init_lock` around the bootstrap block (line 195) is load-bearing. The existing `test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection` calls `get_checkpointer()` twice sequentially.

Let me confirm the test file has no threaded checkpoint test, and PoC whether a threaded test would actually be discriminating.

input.description: Search for any threaded test in persistence tests

content.text:
The PoC confirms the assessor's claim is technically accurate and the suggested test would be discriminating:
- WITH the real `_init_lock`: setup ran exactly 1 time under 8 racing threads.
- WITHOUT the lock (simulated, widened window): setup ran 8 times — clear corruption of the once-per-conn invariant.

So the lock is genuinely load-bearing, and the existing sequential test (`test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection`) does not exercise the concurrency claim documented at checkpoint.py:22-23 and 195. There is no threaded test guarding it (confirmed by grep — no thread/Barrier anywhere in test_persistence.py), unlike the provenance gold-standard at test_provenance.py:170.

Now I'll calibrate severity honestly. This is a test-quality gap, not a production code defect. The production code is correct (the lock works, as my PoC shows). The claim is solely "a test does not demonstrate the lock matters." The original assessor rated it LOW, which is appropriate — possibly even on the borderline of NOT_A_BUG since no shipping behavior is broken. But the task explicitly asks me to report what pass-1 missed, and a missing discriminating test for a load-bearing concurrency guard is a real (if minor) test-coverage gap. Given the engine is being hardened for production and the lock genuinely matters, LOW is the right severity.

input.reasoning:
VERIFIED TRUE, severity LOW (test-coverage gap, not a production code defect).

The production code is correct: the `with _init_lock:` guard wrapping the bootstrap check-and-set (checkpoint.py:195-211) is genuinely load-bearing. My threaded PoC against the fake backend (8 threads released by a Barrier, with a small sleep inside FakeSaver.setup to widen the check->set window) shows: real get_checkpointer() runs setup() exactly ONCE; the same logic with the lock removed runs setup() 8 TIMES. So the documented concurrency claim (checkpoint.py:22-23 'an in-process lock making the check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup') is real and the lock matters.

The assessor's narrow claim is accurate: the only test of this behavior, test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection (test_persistence.py:429-437), calls get_checkpointer() twice SEQUENTIALLY. That exercises only the once-per-conn dedup (_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS membership), which would pass even with the lock removed, because there is no thread interleaving. Confirmed via grep that there is NO threaded/Barrier test anywhere in test_persistence.py — unlike the provenance suite, whose test_record_is_thread_safe_under_concurrency (test_provenance.py:170-201) injects a sleeping clock to widen the race and is documented-verified discriminating. So the checkpoint _init_lock has no test proving it is load-bearing, a gap pass-1 missed.

This is NOT a regression introduced by pass-1 and NOT a shipping-behavior bug (the code works). It is purely a missing discriminating test for a concurrency-critical guard. Severity LOW is correct and matches the original assessor; it is on the NOT_A_BUG/LOW boundary, but for a production-hardening engine a missing test for a load-bearing concurrency lock is a legitimate (minor) defect worth recording. The suggested fix (threaded get_checkpointer() test with a widened setup() window, verified to fail when `with _init_lock:` at line 195 is removed) is exactly right — my PoC is essentially that test and demonstrates it discriminates.

content.text:
Verified the pass-2 assessor's finding as TRUE at LOW severity. Summary:

- The production code is correct — the `_init_lock` guard at `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py:195` is genuinely load-bearing. My threaded PoC shows real `get_checkpointer()` runs `setup()` exactly once under 8 racing threads, vs 8 times with the lock removed.
- The test gap is real: `test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection` (test_persistence.py:429-437) only calls `get_checkpointer()` sequentially and would pass even without the lock. Confirmed via grep that no threaded test exists anywhere in test_persistence.py, unlike the discriminating provenance concurrency test (test_provenance.py:170).
- This is a missing-test defect, not a code bug or a pass-1 regression. LOW severity is correct, and the assessor's suggested fix (threaded test verified discriminating by removing the lock) is sound — my PoC is effectively that test.
